title: Cultural Excellence
paragraphs: Human customs, language, behavior, and life are abstractly directed by certain traditions. To conduct daily life, humans are bound together in the form of family and society. Marriage and traditions establish relationships, creating families. Birth, death, food, clothing, festivals, language, and living styles all fall under cultural characteristics. These dimensions of civilization and culture are key pillars of human identity. Cultural activities help people lead a refined life, making society organized and dynamic.

According to the 2021 National Census, 113,028 people, representing 68.7 percent, are followers of Hinduism in the sub-metropolis. Similarly, 48,251 people (29.3 percent) follow Islam, 1,154 people (0.7 percent) follow Buddhism, and 1,432 people (0.9 percent) follow Christianity. Followers living here celebrate various festivals including Dashain, Tihar, Ram Navami, Maha Shivaratri, Haritalika, Shree Panchami, Krishna Janmashtami, Holi, Chaite Dashain, Saune-Maghe Sankranti, Mata-Tirtha Aunsi, Akshaya Tritiya, Harisayani-Haribodhini Ekadashi, Naga Panchami, Rakshabandhan, Kushe Aunsi, Balachaturdashi, Kojagrat Purnima, Swasthani Purnima, New Year, Sola Shradha, Chhath, Christmas, Eid, and Bakra-Eid.

People of different castes, religions, and attire live in this sub-metropolis. Every caste and community has its own religion, culture, and customs. The Muslim and Chhetri communities are particularly prominent. During festivals, Chhetris traditionally wear Ganyu, Cholo, and Patuka, while men wear Bhoto, Istakot, and Dhaka Topi. Women of the Muslim community traditionally wear Saree, Blouse, Kurta-Suruwal, Burqa, and Hijab, while men wear White Jubba or Kurta-Suruwal. However, in modern times, young people from all communities increasingly prefer wearing pants, shirts, T-shirts, and modern Kurta-Salwars.
